message:
fix(core): address 5 correctness bugs across runtime, text, and widgets

This commit fixes bugs found during review that could cause incorrect
behavior, memory leaks, or panics under specific conditions.

1. EProcess throttle lambda_max formula (eprocess_throttle.rs):
   The upper bound was computed as 1/(1-mu_0) when it should be 1/mu_0.
   The old formula yielded negative lambda_max for mu_0 > 1, allowing
   invalid betting fractions that broke the e-process martingale.

2. Input fairness guard ignoring yield signal (input_fairness.rs):
   check_fairness() always returned should_process: true, completely
   bypassing the yield_to_input starvation protection. During rapid
   resize events, this allowed render work to starve input processing
   indefinitely.

3. Render thread grapheme pool memory leak (render_thread.rs):
   The dedicated render thread loop never called writer.gc(), so
   GraphemePool entries accumulated without bound for long-running
   apps. Added periodic GC every 1000 iterations, matching the
   main event loop behavior.

4. Word navigation skipping whitespace incorrectly (cursor.rs):
   move_word_left and move_word_right did not account for whitespace
   between words. Left navigation now skips trailing spaces before
   finding the word boundary; right navigation skips to the start
   of the next word (past any intervening spaces), matching standard
   editor behavior (Vim w/b, VS Code Ctrl+Left/Right).

5. Markup parser stack overflow on deep nesting (markup.rs):
   Added a DepthLimitExceeded error and a 50-level nesting cap in
   push_tag() to prevent stack overflow from pathological input
   like 10,000 nested bold tags.

6. Integer truncation in list/table width calculation (list.rs,
   table.rs): unicode-width returns usize but the layout code cast
   to u16 with `as u16`, silently truncating lines wider than 65535
   columns. Replaced with saturating cast via .min(u16::MAX as usize).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
